hadiths.chapter: 44. THE CALL TO PRAYER AND THE ESTABLISHMENT OF
hadiths.ar: 883 - وَّق َّال عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم .
hadiths.en:
Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir {a.s} said:  
"For the mu'adhdhin (caller to prayer), Allah {SWT} forgives him as far as his sight and 
voice reach into the sky. Every moist and dry thing that hears him bears witness to his 
truth. For every person who prays with him in his mosque, he receives a share, and for 
every person who prays due to hearing his call, he earns a good deed." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 44, H. 882

hadiths.chapter: 44. THE CALL TO PRAYER AND THE ESTABLISHMENT OF
hadiths.ar:
884  -  وَّ رُّوِى إَّلْمَّلاَّن ِكَّه َّ إ ِذ َّإ سَّمِعَّث ِ إَّلْأ َّذ َّإن َّ مِن ْ إ َّهْلِ إَّلْأ َّرْض ِ ق َّالَّث ْ هَّذ ِةِ إ َّصْوَّإب ُّ إ ُّم َّه ِ مُّحَّم َّذٍ صَّل َّى إَّلل َّهُّ  عَّلَّي ْهِ وَّ 
إ لِهِ ن ِي َّوْجِي ذِ إَّلل َّهِ ق َّي َّسْي َّع ْق ِزُّون َّ إَّلل َّهَّ لِأ ُّم َّه ِ مُّحَّم َّذٍ صَّل َّى إَّلل َّهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ وَّ إ لِهِ جَّي  َّى ي َّق ْ زُّع ُّوإ مِن ْ ي ِلْك َّ إَّلص َّلاَّة ِ .
hadiths.en:
Imam {a.s} said:  
"Whoever calls the adhan (call to prayer) for seven years with the intention of seeking 
Allah’s {SWT} reward will come on the Day of Judgment without any sins." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 286 • Thawab al-A’mal wa Iqab al-A’mal, Part 1, p. 31 • 
Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Part 2, p. 283 • Al -Wafi, Vol. 7, p. 567 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 5, p. 371 • Wasail Al-Shia, 
Vol. 5, p. 374 • Bihar Al Anwar, Vol. 81, p. 146 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 81, p. 149 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 44, H. 883

hadiths.chapter: 44. THE CALL TO PRAYER AND THE ESTABLISHMENT OF
hadiths.ar:
885  -  ورَّوَّى ر ُّرَّإرَّة ُّ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  ج َّعْق َّزٍ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ إ َّن  َّهُّ ق َّال ي َّي ِحَّ إَّلل َّي ْلَّ ن ِا َّذ َّإن ٍ وَّ 
إ ِق َّامَّه ٍ وَّ ي َّق ْي َّي ِحَّ إَّلن  َّهَّارَّ ن ِا َّذ َّإن ٍ وَّ إ ِق َّامَّه ٍ وَّ ي ُّح ْز ِي ك َّ ق ِى  سَّان ِزِ إَّلص َّلاَّة ِ إ ِق َّ امَّه   ن ِع َّي ْزِ إ َّذ َّإن ٍ . 
وج مغ رسول إلله صلى إلله علي ه وإ له ن ي ن  إلط هز وإلعصز ن عزق ه  ن ا ذ إن  وإجذ وإ ق امي ي ن ، وج مغ ن ي ن  إلمع زب  وإلعس اء ن ح مغ 
ن ا ذ إن  وإجذ وإ ق امي ي ن .
hadiths.en:
It is narrated: "When the angels hear the adhan (call to prayer) from the people 
of the earth, they say,  
'These are the voices of the nation of Muhammad {saws} declaring oneness of Allah {SWT}.'  
They then seek forgiveness from Allah {SWT} for the nation of Muhammad until the prayer 
is completed." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 286 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 5, p. 376 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 44, H. 884

hadiths.chapter: 44. THE CALL TO PRAYER AND THE ESTABLISHMENT OF
hadiths.ar:
886  -  وَّ رَّوَّى عَّن ْذُّ إَّلل َّهِ ن ْن ُّ سِن َّان ٍ عَّن ِ إَّلص َّاذِق ِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم :  «
وَّ إَّلْعَّصْزِ ن ِا َّذ َّإن ٍ وَّ إ ِق َّامَّي َّي ْن ِ وَّ ج َّمَّغَّ ن َّي ْن َّ إَّلْمَّع ْزِب ِ وَّ إَّلْعِس َّاءِ ق ِى  إَّلْحَّص َّزِ مِن ْ ع َّي ْزِ عِل َّه ٍ ن ِا ذ َّإن ٍ وَّإجِذٍ وَّ إ ِق َّامَّي َّي ْن ِ .
hadiths.en:
Zurarah narrated from Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al -Baqir {a.s} that He said:  
"The minimum that suffices for the adhan is to begin the night with an adhan and 
iqamah and to begin the day with an adhan and iqamah. For the other prayers during 
the day, an iqamah without an adhan suffices." 
[AL SADUQ] 
The Messenger of Allah {saws}
 combined the Dhuhr (noon) and 'Asr (afternoon) prayers 
at 'Arafat with one adhan and two iqamah, and he combined the Maghrib (sunset) and 
'Isha (night) prayers at Muzdalifah with one adhan and two iqamah.  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 286 • Awaali al-La’aali, Part 3, p. 80 • Awaali al-La’aali, 
Part 4, p. 16 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 7, p. 605 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 5, p. 386 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 44, H. 885

hadiths.chapter: 44. THE CALL TO PRAYER AND THE ESTABLISHMENT OF
hadiths.ar:
887   -  وَّ رُّوِى صَّل َّى ن ِا َّذ َّإن ٍ وَّ إ ِق َّامَّه ٍ صَّل َّى ج َّلْق َّهُّ صَّق  َّان ِ مِن َّ إَّلْمَّلاَّن ِكَّه ِ وَّ مَّن ْ صَّل َّى ن ِا ِق َّامَّه ٍ ن ِع َّي ْزِ إ َّذ َّإن ٍ صَّل َّ ى  
ج َّلْق َّهُّ صَّف    وَّإجِذ  وَّ جَّذ ُّ إَّلص َّف ِّ مَّا ن َّي ْن َّ إَّلْمَّس ْزِق ِ وَّ إَّلْمَّع ْزِب ِ .
hadiths.en:
Abdullah ibn Sinan narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} that:  
"The Messenger of Allah {saws} combined the Dhuhr (noon) and 'Asr (afternoon) prayers 
with one adhan and two iqamah, and he combined the Maghrib (sunset) and 'Isha 
(night) prayers in the same setting without any particular reason, using one adhan and 
two iqamah”. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 287 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 7, p. 282 • Wasail Al-Shia, Part 4, p. 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 44, H. 886

hadiths.chapter: 44. THE CALL TO PRAYER AND THE ESTABLISHMENT OF
hadiths.ar:
888  -  وَّ ق ِى  رِوَّإي َّه ِ إَّلْعَّن  َّاسِ ن ْن ِ هِلاَّلٍ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  إَّلْحَّسَّن ِ إَّلزِّص َّا عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ إ َّن  َّه ق َّال 
صَّق  َّان ِ مِن َّ إَّلْمَّلاَّن ِكَّه ِ وَّ إ ِن ْ إ َّق َّامَّ ن ِع َّي ْزِ إ َّذ َّإن ٍ صَّل َّى عَّن ْ ي َّمِي ن ِهِ وَّإجِذ  وَّ عَّن ْ س ِمَّالِهِ وَّإجِذ  ق َّال .
hadiths.en:
It is narrated: "Whoever prays with both the adhan (call to prayer) and iqamah 
(second call to prayer), two rows of angels pray behind him. And whoever prays with 
just the iqamah without the adhan, one row of angels prays behind him, and the extent 
of the row is from the east to the west." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 287 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 5, p. 382 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 44, H. 887

hadiths.chapter: 44. THE CALL TO PRAYER AND THE ESTABLISHMENT OF
hadiths.ar:
889 - وَّ ق ِى رِوَّإي َّه ِ إِن ْن ِ إ َّن ِى  لَّي ْلَّى عَّن ْ عَّلِى ٍّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ إ َّن  َّهُّ ق َّال لْق َّهُّ صَّق  َّان ِ مِن َّ 
إَّلْمَّلاَّن ِكَّه ِ لأَّ ي ُّزَّى طَّزَّق َّاهُّمَّا وَّ مَّن ْ صَّل َّى ن ِا ِق َّامَّه ٍ صَّل َّى ج َّلْق َّهُّ مَّلَّك   .
hadiths.en:
In the narration of Abbas ibn Hilal from Abu Al -Hasan Imam al-Ridha {a.s} said:  
"Whoever gives the adhan (call to prayer) and the iqamah (second call to prayer) will 
have two rows of angels praying behind him.  
If he only recites the iqamah without the adhan, then one angel will pray on his right 
and one on his left."  
The Imam {a.s} then said: "Take advantage of the two rows (of angels)." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 287 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 7, p. 559 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 5, p. 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 44, H. 888

hadiths.chapter: 44. THE CALL TO PRAYER AND THE ESTABLISHMENT OF
hadiths.ar:
890  -  وَّق َّال إَّلص َّاذِق ُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ :  «-  إَّلل َّهُّم َّ إ ِن ِّى  إ َّسْا َّلُّك َّ ن ِا ِق ْن َّالِ ن َّهَّارِك َّ وَّ إ ِذْن َّارِ 
لَّي ْلِك َّ وَّ جُّص ُّورِ صَّلَّوَّإي ِك َّ وَّ إ َّصْوَّإب ِ ذُّعَّاي ِك َّ إ َّن ْ ي َّي ُّوب َّ عَّلَّى  وَّق َّالَّ مِي ْلَّ ذ َّلِك َّ جِي ن َّ ي َّسْمَّغُّ  
إ َّذ َّإن َّ إَّلْمَّع ْزِب ِ ي ُّم َّ مَّاب َّ مِن ْ ي َّوْمِهِ إ َّوْ لَّي ْلَّي ِهِ مَّاب َّ ي َّان ِن اً وَّ كَّان َّ إِن ْن ُّ إَّلن  َّن  َّاحِ ي َّق ُّولُّ ق ِى  إ َّذ َّإن ِ هِ  -  جَّى  َّ عَّلَّى ج َّي ْزِ إَّلْعَّمَّلِ جَّى  َّ 
عَّلَّى ج َّي ْزِ إَّلْعَّمَّلِ ق َّا ِذ َّإ رَّإ ةُّ عَّلِى    عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّ لاَّمُّ ق َّال زْجَّن اً ن ِالْق َّان ِلِي ن َّ عَّذْلأً وَّ ن ِالص َّلاَّة ِ مَّزْجَّن اً وَّ إ َّهْلاً .
hadiths.en:
In a narration from Ibn Abi Layla, Imam Ali {a.s} said:  
"Whoever prays with both the adhan (call to prayer) and the iqamah (second call to 
prayer) will have two rows of angels praying behind him, the ends of which cannot be 
seen.  
And whoever prays with only the iqamah will have an angel praying behind him."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 287 • Awaali al-La’aali, Part 1, p. 352 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 7, 
p. 559 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 5, p. 382 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 81, p. 147 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 44, H. 889

hadiths.chapter: 44. THE CALL TO PRAYER AND THE ESTABLISHMENT OF
hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} said:  
"Whoever, upon hearing the adhan for Fajr (morning prayer), says:  
'O’ Allah {SWT}, I ask You by the coming of Your day and the departure of Your night, and 
by the presence of Your prayers and the voices of Your callers, that You accept my 
repentance. Indeed, You are the Most Accepting of Repentance, the Most Merciful,' 
and then says a similar supplication upon hearing the adhan for Maghrib (evening 
prayer), and dies during that day or night, dies in a state of repentance."  
He also mentioned that Ibn al-Nabbah used to say during his adhan, "Hayya 'ala khayr 
al-'Amal (Hasten to the best of deeds), hasten to the best of deeds," and whenever 
Imam Ali {a.s} would see him, Imam {a.s} would say,  
"Welcome, O’ caller of justice, and welcome to prayer."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 44, H. 890

hadiths.chapter: 44. THE CALL TO PRAYER AND THE ESTABLISHMENT OF
hadiths.ar:
892  -  وَّق َّال إ َّن ُّو ج َّعْق َّزٍ لِمُّحَّم َّذِ ن ْن ِ مُّسْلِمٍ :  «
ي ُّن َّاذِى  ن ِالْأ َّذ َّإن ِ وَّ إ َّن ْث َّ عَّلَّى إَّلْح َّلاَّءِ ق َّاذ ْكُّزِ إَّلل َّهَّ عَّز  َّ وَّ ج َّل َّ وَّ ق ُّلْ كَّمَّا ي َّق ُّولُّ إَّلْمُّو َّذ ِّن .
hadiths.en:
Harith ibn al-Mughira al-Nadri narrated from Imam Al-Sadiq {a.s} that He said: 
"Whoever hears the mu'adhdhin (caller to prayer) say, 'I bear witness that there is no 
god but Allah {SWT}, and I bear witness that Muhammad is the Messenger of Allah {saws},' 
and then, affirming and seeking reward, responds with  
'And I also bear witness that there is no god but Allah {SWT}, and I bear witness that 
Muhammad is the Messenger of Allah {saws}. I suffice myself with these testimonies 
against anyone who denies or rejects, and I support with them anyone who affirms and 
bears witness,' will receive a reward equivalent to the number of those who deny and 
reject and the number of those who affirm and bear witness."  
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, Volume 3, p. 307 • Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 288 • Thawab al -A’mal wa 
Iqab al-A’mal, Part 1, p. 31 • Al -Amali (by Al-Saduq) Vol. 1, p. 215 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 7, p. 564 • Wasail Al-Shia, 
Vol. 5, p. 454 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 81, p. 175 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 44, H. 891

hadiths.chapter: 44. THE CALL TO PRAYER AND THE ESTABLISHMENT OF
hadiths.ar:
893   -  وَّ سَّا َّلَّ ر َّي ْذ  إَّلس  َّح َّامُّ إ َّن َّا عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ :  عَّن ْ رَّج ُّلٍ ن َّسِى َّ إَّلْأ َّذ َّإن َّ وَّ إَّلْأ ِق َّامَّه َّ جَّي  َّى ذَّج َّلَّ ق ِى  إَّلص َّلاَّة ِ ق َّق َّالَّ  
«ق ِزَّإءَّة ِ ق َّلْي ُّي ِم َّ صَّلاَّي َّه
hadiths.en:
Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir {a.s} said to Muhammad ibn Muslim:  
"O’ Muhammad ibn Muslim, never neglect the remembrance of Allah {SWT} in any 
situation. Even if you hear the caller calling the adhan while you are in a state of 
relieving yourself, remember Allah {SWT}, the Mighty and Majestic, and say what the 
mu'adhdhin says." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 288 • Ilal al -Sharai’ Vol. 1, p. 284 • Al -Wafi, Vol. 7, p. 
564 • Wasail Al-Shia, Part 1, p. 314 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 5, p. 454 • Bihar Al Anwar, Vol. 77, p. 175 • Bihar 
Al-Anwar, Vol. 81, p. 176 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 44, H. 892

hadiths.chapter: 44. THE CALL TO PRAYER AND THE ESTABLISHMENT OF
hadiths.ar:
894  -  ورُّوِى َّ عَّن ْ عَّم َّارٍ إَّلس َّان َّاطِى ِّ إ َّن  َّهُّ ق َّالَّ: سُّن ِلَّ إ َّن ُّو عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ عَّن ْ رَّج ُّلٍ ن َّسِى َّ مِن َّ   إَّلْأ َّذ َّإن ِ جَّزْق اً ق َّذ َّكَّزَّةُّ 
جِي ن َّ ق َّزَّع َّ مِن َّ إَّلْأ َّذ َّإن ِ وَّ إَّلْأ ِق َّامَّه ِ ق َّال لْي َّق ُّلْهُّ وَّ لْي َّق ُّلْ مِن ْ ذ َّلِك َّ إَّلْحَّزْف ِ إ ِلَّى إ ج ِزِةِ وَّ لأَّ 
ي ُّعِي ذُّ إَّلْأ َّذ َّإن َّ كُّل َّهُّ وَّ لأَّ إَّلْأ ِق َّامَّه َّ .
hadiths.en:
Zaid al-Shahham asked Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s} about a man 
who forgot the adhan (call to prayer) and iqamah (second call to prayer) and began the 
prayer.  
The Imam said: "If he remembers before reciting (the Qur'an in prayer), he should send 
blessings upon the Prophet and his family and then recite the iqamah. But if he has 
already started reciting, then he should complete his prayer”.  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 44, H. 893

hadiths.chapter: 44. THE CALL TO PRAYER AND THE ESTABLISHMENT OF
hadiths.ar:
895  -  وَّ سَّا َّلَّ مُّعَّاوِي َّه ُّ ن ْن ُّ وَّهْث ٍ إ َّن َّا عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم :  عَّن ِ إَّلي  َّي ْوِي ث ِ إَّل َّذ ِى  ي َّكُّون ُّ ن َّي ْن َّ إَّلْأ َّذ َّإن ِ وَّ إَّلْأ ِق َّامَّه ِ ق َّق َّال 
ن َّعْزِق ُّه .
hadiths.en:
It was narrated from Ammar al -Sabati that He said: "Imam Jafar ibn 
Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} was asked about a man who forgot a word during the adhan 
(call to prayer) and remembered it after completing the adhan and iqamah (second 
call to prayer).  
The Imam said: "He should go back to the word he forgot, say it, and then continue 
from that point to the end. He does not need to repeat the entire adhan or the iqamah." 
[REFERENCES] Al-Wafi, Vol. 7, p. 623 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 5, p. 442 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 44, H. 894

hadiths.chapter: 44. THE CALL TO PRAYER AND THE ESTABLISHMENT OF
hadiths.en:
Muawiya ibn Wahb asked Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s} about the 
practice of "tathwib" (additional announcements or calls made between the adhan 
and the iqamah).  
Imam {a.s} replied: "We do not recognize it”. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 44, H. 895

hadiths.chapter: 44. THE CALL TO PRAYER AND THE ESTABLISHMENT OF
hadiths.ar:
897   -  وَّ رَّوَّى إ َّن ُّو ن َّكْزٍ إَّلْحَّص ْزَّمِى  ُّ وَّ كُّلَّي ْث إَّلْأ َّسَّذِى  ُّ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ :  إ َّن  َّهُّ جَّكَّى لَّهُّمَّا إَّلْأ َّذ َّإن َّ ق َّق َّال -  
إَّلل َّهُّ إ َّكْن َّزُّ إَّلل َّهُّ إ َّكْن َّزُّ إَّلل َّهُّ إ َّكْن َّزُّ إَّلل َّهُّ إ َّكْن َّزُّ إ َّس ْهَّذُّ إ َّن ْ لأَّ إ ِلَّهَّ إ ِلأ َّ إَّلل َّهُّ إ َّس ْهَّذُّ إ َّن ْ  لأَّ إ ِلَّهَّ إ ِلأ َّ إَّلل َّهُّ إ َّس ْهَّذُّ إ َّن  َّ مُّحَّم َّذإً رَّسُّولُّ إَّلل َّهِ إ َّس ْهَّذُّ  
إ َّن  َّ  مُّحَّم َّذإً رَّسُّولُّ إَّلل َّهِ جَّى  َّ عَّلَّى إَّلص َّلاَّة ِ جَّى  َّ عَّلَّى إَّلص َّلاَّة ِ جَّى  َّ عَّلَّى إَّلْق َّلاَّحِ جَّى  َّ عَّلَّى إَّلْق َّلاَّحِ   جَّى  َّ عَّلَّى ج َّي ْزِ إَّلْعَّمَّلِ 
جَّى  َّ عَّلَّى ج َّي ْزِ إَّلْعَّمَّلِ إَّلل َّهُّ  إ َّكْن َّزُّ إَّلل َّهُّ إ َّكْن َّزُّ لأَّ إ ِلَّهَّ إ ِلأ َّ إَّلل َّهُّ لأَّ إ ِلَّهَّ إ ِلأ َّ إَّلل َّه .    
ولأ ن ا س إ ن  ي ق ال ق ى  صلاة  إلع ذإة  على إ ي ز جى  على ج ي ز إلعمل " إلصلاة  ج ي ز من  إلن وم " مزي ي ن  للي ق ي ه .  
وق ال مصن ف  هذ إ إلكي اب    رجمه إلله: هذ إ هو  إلأذ إن  إلصحي ح لأ ي ز إذ ق ي ه ولأ ي ن ق ض من ه، وإلمق وص ه  لعن هم إلله ق ذ وص عوإ  
إ ج ن ارإ ور إذوإ ق ى  إلأذ إن  " محمذ وإ ل محمذ ج ي ز إلن زي ه  " مزي ي ن ، وق ى  ن عض  روإي اي هم ن عذ إ س هذ إ ن  محمذإ رسول إلله "  
إ س هذ إ ن  علي ا ولى  إلله " مزي ي ن ، ومن هم من  روى ن ذل ذ لك  " إ س هذ إ ن  علي ا إ مي ز إلمو من ي ن   جق ا " مزي ي ن  ولأ س ك  ق ى  إ ن  علي ا 
ولى  إلله وإ ن ه إ مي ز إلمو من ي ن  جق ا وإ ن  محمذإ وإ له صلوإب  إلله علي هم ج ي ز إلن زي ه ، ولكن  لي س ذ لك  ق ى  إ صل إلأذ إن ، وإ ن ما 
ذ كزب  ذ لك  لي عزف  ن هذ ة إلز ي اذة  إلمي همون  ن الي ق وي ض ، إلمذلسون  إ ن ق سهم ق ى  ج ملي ن ا .
hadiths.en:
Imam Ali {a.s} used to say:  
"There is no issue if a boy calls the adhan (call to prayer) before reaching the age of 
puberty, and there is no issue if the mu'adhdhin (caller to prayer) is in a state of 
janabah (major ritual impurity), but he should not perform the iqamah (second call to 
prayer) until he has performed ghusl (ritual purification)."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 289 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Part 2, p. 53 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 7, 
p. 595 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 44, H. 896

hadiths.chapter: 44. THE CALL TO PRAYER AND THE ESTABLISHMENT OF
hadiths.ar: 898 - وَّق َّال إَّلص َّاذِق ُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ : ق ِى  إَّلْمُّو َّذ ِّن ِي ن .
hadiths.en:
Abu Bakr al -Hadhrami and Kulaib al -Asadi narrated from Imam Jafar ibn 
Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} that he taught them the adhan, saying:  
"Allahu Akbar, Allahu Akbar, Allahu Akbar, Allahu Akbar (Allah {SWT} is the Greatest, 
repeated four times). 
I bear witness that there is no god but Allah {SWT} (twice).  
I bear witness that Muhammad is the Messenger of Allah {saws} (twice).  
Hasten to prayer (twice).  
Hasten to success (twice).  
Hasten to the best of deeds (twice).  
Allahu Akbar, Allahu Akbar. There is no god but Allah {SWT} (twice)." 
The iqamah is the same.  
[AL SADUQ] 
It is also permissible, in the Fajr prayer, after "Hasten to the best of deeds" to say 
"Prayer is better than sleep" twice, for reasons of taqiyya (precautionary 
dissimulation). 
The author of this book, may Allah {SWT} have mercy on him, commented:  
This is the correct adhan; nothing should be added to or removed from it. The 
"Mufawwida" (a sect accused of exaggeration), may Allah {SWT} curse them, have 
fabricated reports and added phrases to the adhan such as "Muhammad and the 
family of Muhammad are the best of creation" repeated twice, or "I bear witness that 
Ali is the wali (guardian) of Allah {SWT}" repeated twice, and in some reports "I bear 
witness that Ali is truly The Commander of the Faithful" repeated twice. There is no 
doubt that Ali is the wali of Allah {SWT} and the true Commander of the Faithful, and that 
Muhammad and his family {a.s} are the best of creation. However, these additions are 
not part of the original adhan, and I have mentioned them here so that these 
fabrications and those accused of exaggeration who mix themselves among us may 
be recognized. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 289 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Part 2, p. 60 • Al-Istibsar, Part 
1, p. 306 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 5, p. 416 • Mustadrak Al-Wasail, Vol. 4, p. 40 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 44, H. 897

hadiths.chapter: 44. THE CALL TO PRAYER AND THE ESTABLISHMENT OF
hadiths.ar:
899 - وَّق َّال عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم .   
وي ن ن ع ى  إ ن  ي كون  ن ي ن  إلأ ذ إن  وإلأ ق امه  ج لسه  إ لأ إلمع زب  ق ا ن ه ي ح ز ى .
hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s} said regarding the mu'adhdhins 
(callers to prayer):  
"They are the trustworthy ones”. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 291 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 7, p. 617 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 5, p. 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 44, H. 898

hadiths.chapter: 44. THE CALL TO PRAYER AND THE ESTABLISHMENT OF
hadiths.ar: 900  -  وَّ رَّوَّى عَّن ْذُّ إَّلز َّجْمَّن ِ ن ْن ُّ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّن ِ إَّلص َّاذِق ِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ إ َّن  َّهُّ ق َّال س َّق َّزِ إ ِق َّامَّه   ن ِع َّي ْزِ إ َّذ َّإن ٍ .
hadiths.en:
Imam {a.s} said:  
"Pray the Jumu'ah (Jumu’ah prayer) with the adhan of these people, for they are the 
most diligent in observing the time." 
[AL SADUQ] 
There should be a sitting between the adhan and iqamah, except for Maghrib, where it 
suffices to have only a breath's interval between the adhan and iqamah.  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 291 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Part 2, p. 284 • Wasail Al-Shia, 
Vol. 5, p. 378 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 44, H. 899

hadiths.chapter: 44. THE CALL TO PRAYER AND THE ESTABLISHMENT OF
hadiths.ar:
901   -  وَّ رَّوَّى إ َّن ُّو ن َّصِي زٍ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ إ َّن  َّهُّ ق َّال ى   ن َّي ْي ِك َّ ي ُّم َّ إ َّق َّمْث َّ ق ِى   
إَّلْمَّسْح ِذِ إ َّج ْز َّإ َّك َّ .
hadiths.en:
Abd al-Rahman ibn Abi Abdullah narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-
Sadiq {a.s} that He said:  
"In travel, the iqamah (second call to prayer) suffices without the adhan (call to 
prayer)." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 291 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 7, p. 605 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 5, p. 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 44, H. 900

hadiths.chapter: 44. THE CALL TO PRAYER AND THE ESTABLISHMENT OF
hadiths.en:
Abu Basir narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s} that He said:  
"If you give the adhan (call to prayer) on the road or in your home, and then perform 
the iqamah (second call to prayer) in the mosque, it suffices."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 291 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 7, p. 598 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 5, p. 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 44, H. 901
